name: Printing As A Service with Metering

annotation.text:
This example stream showcases the Printing As A Service use case solution using the Usage metering extension.
For more information on Usage metering, see https://infozone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DAZ/pages/79954163/Usage+metering.

In this use case, where instead of selling printers as products, AlphaPrint sells printing as a service and charges its customers based on actual usage.
In this example stream, you will learn:

How to configure your products as meter types.

How to configure your stream using meter processors.

How to create a meter when a customer installs a new printer.

This example stream also demonstrates how to collect different usage events such as the number of printed pages, ink level, and printer health status.
After collecting the data, the stream shows how to meter the collected usage per printer, printer ink, and health status before sending it to its final destination, in this case, the Log function.

This example requires you to run Helper streams before running the main stream. 

See the setup guide: https://infozone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DAZ/pages/228687874/Printing+as+a+Service+-+Metering+Setup+Guide

Renames the input data to match the format required by the downstream system. Then stores the data in a log. 

This step substitutes data being sent to an ink replenishment ordering system.

Generate sample records with printer data (printed pages, ink level, printers' health status, and so on).
This step substitutes real data input.

Generates sample records with printer data (printed pages, ink level, printer health status, etc.).
This step substitutes real data input.

This part checks the printers' health status and sends a request for maintenance if needed.

The Route function checks the printer health status (normal or maintenance required). Then the Deduplicate function filters out duplicated records to ensure the 'health status check' event is only processed once per printer per day.
Simulates enriching the usage data summaries with the customer and product master data from an external system.

Filters out erroneous and duplicate records. 
All records that are filtered by the Validate function can be further processed and corrected using Data Correction.

Renames the input data as desired. Then stores the data in a log. 

This step substitutes for sending a maintenance service order.

Renames the input data fields to match those required by the Meter processor.

The Meter processor sums the monthly amount of printed paper sheets per printer. All records that are filtered by the Meter processor function can be further processed and corrected using Data Correction.
Simulates enriching the usage data summaries with the customer and product master data from an external system.

Renames the input data to match the format required by the downstream system. Then, stores the data in a log. 

This step substitutes data being sent to a billing system.

This step checks the daily ink level per customer and triggers an ink order if needed.

The Route function checks the ink level. If the "low ink" threshold is reached, the Deduplicate function filters out any duplicated "low ink alert" records, ensuring that only one 'low ink' event is processed daily.
Simulates enriching the usage data summaries with the customer and product master data from an external system.
